name: memnexus
description: MemNexus Code Memory - Persistent memory for AI coding sessions

tools.name: memory_search
tools.description: Search project memory for relevant context. Use this when you need to understand: 1) What was discussed in previous sessions, 2) Recent changes to a file/module, 3) Design decisions or architecture, 4) How something was implemented

query.description: Search query describing what you're looking for (e.g., 'auth module implementation', 'recent changes to database', 'why use Redis')

source.description: Source to search: 'git' for commit history, 'code' for code symbols, 'all' for everything

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return

tools.name: memory_store
tools.description: Store important information to project memory. Use this to remember: 1) Key design decisions, 2) Important findings during debugging, 3) Architecture patterns, 4) Workarounds or hacks, 5) Session summaries

content.description: The content to store in memory. Be concise but include key details.

category.description: Category of the memory for better organization

tools.name: memory_status
tools.description: Check MemNexus status for the current project. Shows: 1) Whether project is initialized, 2) Number of indexed commits, 3) Number of code symbols, 4) Last indexed time

tools.name: memory_index
tools.description: Index project into memory. Use this when: 1) First time using MemNexus on a project, 2) After significant code changes, 3) When search returns no results

tools.name: find_symbol
tools.description: Find a specific code symbol (function, class, method) by name. Use this when: 1) Looking for a specific function implementation, 2) Finding where a class is defined, 3) Understanding a method's signature

tools.name: get_file_history
tools.description: Get Git history for a specific file. Use this when: 1) Understanding recent changes to a file, 2) Finding when a bug was introduced, 3) Seeing who modified a file and why

file_path.description: Path to the file (relative to project root, e.g., 'src/auth.py')

limit.description: Maximum number of commits to return
